WebSearch: BBN bound dark sector particle lifetime less than 1 second decaying reheating
WebSearch: Delta Neff dark radiation CMB BBN constraint MeV scale dark sector
WebSearch: NA64 dark photon limit epsilon mA 300-700 MeV
WebSearch: BaBar dark photon invisible monophoton limit epsilon
WebSearch: dark Higgs decay lifetime mixing angle theta scalar portal MeV GeV BBN CMB

WebFetch: ar5iv 2105.04565 dark photon limits handbook
warnings: Kinetic mixing epsilon is unspecified. Most dark photon constraints depend on epsilon, and their applicability requires knowing whether A' decays visibly (to SM) or invisibly (to dark sector particles). Both channels may be open.
Higgs portal lambda_HPhi is unspecified. All constraints on the dark scalar rho depend on the scalar mixing angle sin^2(theta) which is proportional to lambda_HPhi.

The xi=1 assumption (thermal equilibrium of dark and SM sectors) is in tension with Delta N_eff constraints. At temperatures > m_A', m_rho, the dark sector contributes Delta N_eff ~ 0.57, exceeding Planck 95% CL bounds. The model needs either a lower xi or a mechanism to deplete dark sector energy density before BBN/CMB.

The BBN constraint from Coy et al. (2025) gives a lower bound on epsilon (epsilon > 10^{-9}-10^{-8} for m_A' ~ 300-700 MeV) rather than an upper bound, because dark photons must decay before BBN to avoid overclosure. This lower bound must be checked against the epsilon value assumed by the model.

Several constraints classified as 'approximate' or 'recast_needed' require computing observables (A' decay widths and branching fractions, rho lifetime and branching fractions, dark sector abundance at BBN, temperature evolution during PT) that are not available from upstream handoffs.
approximations: Dark photon constraints assume either visible decay (A'->e+e-, mu+mu-) or invisible decay (A'->dark sector). The actual branching ratio depends on the unknown kinetic mixing epsilon and the rho-A' couplings.
BBN/CMB constraints on rho assume freeze-in production via Higgs mixing. The actual abundance depends on the unknown lambda_HPhi.

Delta N_eff estimate assumes the dark sector thermalizes with the SM at T > dark sector mass scale, consistent with the xi=1 assumption. If the dark sector never reaches thermal equilibrium, the Delta N_eff contribution could be much smaller.
LHCb constraint applies for BR(A'->mu+mu-) ~ 1. In the presence of the dark Higgs rho, BR(A'->mu+mu-) < 1 due to the A'->rho+A'* channel (if kinematically allowed).

notes: MAP values: v=1.07 GeV, g_D=0.508, m_A'=0.54 GeV, m_rho=0.054 GeV
Full 1-sigma posterior from PTArcade Bayesian analysis of NG15 data
Preferred region corresponds to very strong supercooling (alpha ~ 10^11)
Portal couplings epsilon and lambda_HPhi are unspecified (assumed small)

constraints.name: LHCb prompt A' -> mu+mu-
constraints.description: LHCb search for A' -> mu+mu- using 5.5/fb of pp collisions at 13 TeV. World-leading constraints for 214 < m_A' < 740 MeV.
constraints.observable: epsilon vs m_A' for visible A' -> mu+mu- decays

conventions.epsilon: LHCb uses epsilon^2 convention. BR(A'->mu+mu-) assumed ~1 for visible search.

constraints.applicability: Applies to entire 1-sigma mA' range (0.31-0.67 GeV). If BR(A'->mu+mu-) ~ 1, excludes epsilon > few x 1e-4. But A' may also decay invisibly to rho+A'*.
applicability_notes: Full strength constraint applies only if A'->mu+mu- BR ~ 1
Invisible decay channel reduces visible BR, weakening constraint
LHCb 2020 result (PRL 124, 041801) is the state-of-the-art for this mass range
Run 3 data (2024-2026) expected to improve sensitivity by ~O(100-1000)

source.title: Search for A' -> mu+mu- Decays

constraints.name: BaBar invisible A' monophoton
constraints.description: BaBar search for e+e- -> gamma A', A' -> invisible. Uses 53/fb at PEP-II. 90% CL limits on epsilon for m_A' < 8 GeV.
constraints.observable: epsilon vs m_A' for invisible A' decays

conventions.epsilon: BaBar uses epsilon convention. 90% CL limits on epsilon^2.

constraints.applicability: Applies if A' predominantly decays invisibly. Excludes epsilon > (2-4)x10^{-4} for mA'=0.3-0.7 GeV.
applicability_notes: Strongest collider constraint for invisible A' decays in this mass range
Competitive with LHCb prompt search if A' decays invisibly
Belle II will improve sensitivity by ~3x over BaBar

source.title: Search for Invisible Decays of a Dark Photon Produced in e+e- Collisions at BaBar

constraints.name: NA64 invisible dark photon (electron beam)
constraints.description: NA64 missing energy search for A' produced via dark bremsstrahlung of 100 GeV electrons. Most sensitive for mA' < 350 MeV.
constraints.observable: epsilon vs m_A' for invisible A' -> DM decays

conventions.epsilon: NA64 uses epsilon convention. 90% CL limits from 9.37e11 EOT.

constraints.applicability: Partially covers 1-sigma region: only lower boundary (mA' ~ 0.31 GeV) overlaps NA64 sensitivity (< 0.35 GeV). Sensitivity drops above 350 MeV.
applicability_notes: Only the lower end of 1-sigma mA' range overlaps NA64 sensitivity
Post-LS3 muon program may extend sensitivity to higher masses

source.title: Search for Light Dark Matter with NA64 at CERN

constraints.name: BBN dark photon decay constraint
constraints.description: Dark photons must decay before BBN (tau' < 0.2 s). Otherwise residual energy density exceeds BBN bounds on expansion rate.
constraints.observable: A' lifetime vs BBN temperature

conventions.epsilon: Coy et al. 2025 convention. Lower bound on epsilon from tau' < 0.2 s.

constraints.applicability: Applies to A' that decays to SM. For m_A' ~ 300-700 MeV, A'->e+e- and A'->hadrons give tau' < 0.2 s requiring epsilon > 10^{-8}. This is a LOWER bound on epsilon.
applicability_notes: BBN gives a lower bound, not upper bound, on epsilon for tau' < 0.2 s
If A' decays predominantly to dark sector (rho, A'*), this constraint may be modified
Assumes A' has a thermal or comparable abundance

source.title: Light from darkness: history of a hot dark sector

constraints.name: BBN dark scalar (rho) decay constraint
constraints.description: Dark scalar rho mixing with Higgs via lambda_HPhi. For sin^2(theta) > few x 10^{-7}, rho decays during BBN affecting element abundances.
constraints.observable: rho lifetime and decay to e+e- vs BBN constraints

conventions.sin^2(theta): Mixing angle with SM Higgs. sin^2(theta) = (lambda_HPhi * v_dark * v_SM / m_h^2)^2

constraints.applicability: BBN constraints on scalars with lifetime tau > 0.1 s. For m_rho=50-100 MeV, decays to e+e- dominate. Excludes sin^2(theta) > 10^{-7} (short lifetime + overproduction) and 10^{-11} < sin^2(theta) < 10^{-7} (long-lived + CMB/BBN).
applicability_notes: Full constraint requires computing rho production (freeze-in) and decay
Different mass regimes have different dominant decay channels
For m_rho < 2m_mu ~ 211 MeV, only e+e- and gamma gamma channels are open
rho -> e+e- leads to electromagnetic energy injection constrained by BBN and CMB

source.title: Cosmological beam dump: constraints on dark scalars mixed with the Higgs boson

constraints.name: CMB energy injection from decaying rho
constraints.description: Planck CMB power spectrum constrains energy injection from decaying particles at z < 1100. For m_rho=50-100 MeV, e+e- final state gives strong constraints.
constraints.observable: CMB anisotropy constraints on decaying particles with tau > 10^13 s

conventions.sin^2(theta): Scalar-Higgs mixing angle. Lifetime scales as tau ~ 10^{14} s * (10^{-10}/theta^2) * (100 MeV/m_rho)

constraints.applicability: CMB excludes sin^2(theta) ~ 10^{-11} to 10^{-10} for m_rho=50-100 MeV decaying to e+e- with tau > 10^13 s. Combined with BBN, the window 10^{-11} < sin^2(theta) < 10^{-7} is excluded.
applicability_notes: CMB constraint is the strongest probe for long-lived particles (tau > 10^13 s)
Effective deposition fraction f_eff ~ 1 for e+e- final state at these masses
Combined BBN+CMB exclusion covers 10^{-11} < theta < 10^{-7}

source.title: Cosmological beam dump: constraints on dark scalars mixed with the Higgs boson

constraints.name: Delta N_eff from dark radiation
constraints.description: Additional relativistic degrees of freedom in the dark sector (A': 3 dofs, rho: 1 dof). With xi=1, Delta N_eff exceeds Planck bounds.
constraints.observable: Delta N_eff = (8/7) * (xi)^4 * (g_dark_eff / g_gamma_eff) * (g*S(T)/g*S(T_dark))^{4/3}

conventions.Delta N_eff: Planck 2018: N_eff = 2.99+/-0.17 -> Delta N_eff < 0.3 at 95% CL

constraints.applicability: If xi=1 and T > m_A',m_rho, dark sector has 4 bosonic dofs giving Delta N_eff ~ 0.57. Exceeds Planck 95% CL bound (0.3). Requires either xi < 1 or non-thermal dark sector.
applicability_notes: This is a serious tension with the xi=1 assumption
If dark sector is not thermalized with SM, xi may be much smaller
After supercooled FOPT, the dark sector reheats but may have negligible abundance at BBN
Need to compute actual dark sector energy density at BBN/CMB epoch

source.title: Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters

constraints.name: Perturbativity bound (g_D)
constraints.description: Gauge coupling must satisfy g_D < sqrt(4*pi) for perturbativity; g_D^2/(4*pi) < 1.
constraints.observable: g_D and g_D^2/(4*pi) < 1

conventions.g_D: Dimensionless gauge coupling. Standard perturbativity: g_D^2/(4*pi) < 1.

constraints.applicability: Satisfied: g_D^2/(4*pi) = 0.082-0.095, well below 1.

source.title: Standard quantum field theory perturbativity criterion

constraints.name: Coleman-Weinberg consistency (beta_lambda > 0)
constraints.description: Radiative symmetry breaking via CW mechanism requires beta_lambda > 0 for the scalar quartic coupling.
constraints.observable: beta_lambda = 3*g_D^4/(8*pi^2)

conventions.beta_lambda: One-loop quartic beta function. Positive = boson-driven CW.

constraints.applicability: Satisfied: beta_lambda = 0.0025-0.0034 > 0. Boson-dominated CW mechanism works.

source.title: Coleman and Weinberg, Phys. Rev. D 7, 1888 (1973)

constraints.name: Scalar mixing from Higgs signal strength
constraints.description: Higgs boson production and decay rates constrain mixing with a new scalar. Exotic Higgs decays h -> rho+rho are possible if m_rho < m_h/2.
constraints.observable: BR(h -> exotic) and Higgs signal strength constraints

conventions.sin^2(theta): Scalar-Higgs mixing. BR(h -> exotic) < 10-20% at 95% CL.

constraints.applicability: For m_rho=50-100 MeV (<< m_h/2), h -> rho+rho is kinematically allowed. Constraint on lambda_HPhi depends on whether rho acquires vev-like coupling to Higgs.
applicability_notes: CMS EXO-24-034 (2025) probes h->SS->mu+mu- h+h- for m_S=0.4-2 GeV
However m_rho=50-71 MeV is below CMS sensitivity range
LEP constraints on light Higgs-like scalar: sin^2(theta) < 0.1-0.2

source.title: Combining LEP and LHC to bound the Higgs Width

constraints.name: Beam dump constraints on dark scalar (CHARM, PS191, LSND)
constraints.description: Fixed-target experiments search for scalars produced in K/pi decays, decaying to e+e- pairs.
constraints.observable: Scalar production in K decays, detected via e+e- decays

conventions.sin^2(theta): Scalar-Higgs mixing angle. PS191 excludes sin^2(theta) ~ 10^{-7}-3x10^{-7} for m=100-150 MeV.

constraints.applicability: Partially covers the 1-sigma m_rho range. PS191 covers 100-150 MeV (partially overlapping 50-71 MeV upper end). CHARM covers 100-200 MeV range.
applicability_notes: PS191 constraint strongest at 100-150 MeV; weaker below 100 MeV
Kaon production threshold: K -> pi + rho requires m_rho < m_K - m_pi ~ 360 MeV (satisfied)
Exact constraint requires computing K->pi+rho production rate

source.title: Constraints on light scalars from PS191 results

constraints.name: Stellar cooling (RGB/HB) from A'
constraints.description: Resonant production of A' in stellar cores modifies energy loss rates. Strongest for m_A' ~ plasma frequency (keV range).
constraints.observable: Stellar energy loss rate from A' production

conventions.epsilon: Dolan et al. (2023) convention. Resonant production when m_A' ~ omega_pl.

constraints.applicability: Not applicable: m_A' = 300-700 MeV >> thermal plasma frequencies in stellar cores (~keV). Production is exponentially suppressed.
applicability_notes: Stellar cooling bound applies for m_A' < O(100 keV)
For m_A' ~ 300-700 MeV, Boltzmann factor exp(-m_A'/T) suppresses production

source.title: Constraining dark photons with self-consistent simulations of globular cluster stars

constraints.name: SN1987A cooling from A'
constraints.description: Traditional SN1987A cooling bound from neutrino signal duration. A' production in SN core carries away energy.
constraints.observable: SN1987A neutrino signal duration constraint on A' cooling
required_inputs: A' production rate in SN core

conventions.epsilon: Standard SN cooling bound applies for m_A' < ~200 MeV

constraints.applicability: Not applicable: m_A' = 300-700 MeV > 200 MeV upper bound. Production in SN core (T_core ~ 30-60 MeV) is Boltzmann-suppressed by exp(-m_A'/T).
applicability_notes: Traditional SN cooling bound: m_A' < 200 MeV
New gain-layer constraint (Caputo 2025) strongest at 0.1-0.4 MeV
Boltzmann factor for m_A'=300 MeV, T=30 MeV: exp(-10) ~ 4e-5, strongly suppressing production

source.title: Cooling the Shock: New Supernova Constraints on Dark Photons

constraints.name: Direct detection (XENONnT, LZ) dark photon absorption
constraints.description: Direct detection experiments search for absorption of dark photon dark matter via electron recoils.
constraints.observable: Dark photon absorption signal in LXe TPC

conventions.epsilon: XENONnT constrains sub-keV dark photon absorption

constraints.applicability: Not applicable: m_A' = 300-700 MeV >> keV range accessible to direct detection via absorption.
applicability_notes: Direct detection probes sub-MeV dark photon via absorption
For MeV-GeV dark photons, collider/beam-dump constraints dominate

source.title: XENONnT Search for Light Dark Matter in Low-Energy Ionization Signals

constraints.name: Delta N_eff from residual dark dofs after PT
constraints.description: After the supercooled FOPT, the dark sector reheats. Residual dark sector particles may contribute to radiation density at BBN/CMB.
constraints.observable: Effective number of relativistic species after PT and reheating
missing_observables: Residual dark particle abundance after reheating

conventions.Delta N_eff: CMB Planck 2018: Delta N_eff < 0.3 at 95% CL. BBN: Delta N_eff < 0.4.

constraints.applicability: The very strong supercooling (alpha ~ 10^11) means the dark sector is extremely cold after PT. Most energy is dumped into GWs and reheating of SM. Residual dark particle abundance may be negligible, but this needs computation.
applicability_notes: The supercooled PT exponentially dilutes dark sector particle number densities
Reheating temperature T_reh ~ O(MeV) from FOPT results
Need to compute the residual dark sector energy density at BBN epoch

source.title: Supercooled phase transitions: why thermal history of hidden sector matters

constraints.name: Classical scale invariance stability (no tree-level mass term)
constraints.description: Model must not have dimensionful parameters in the tree-level Lagrangian. Checked against model definition.
constraints.observable: Absence of tree-level mass term for Phi and A'
available_observables: Model has no tree-level mass term
constraints.applicability: Confirmed: Lagrangian contains only |D_mu Phi|^2 - lambda|Phi|^4. No mass term.

source.title: Model specification (handoff_model.json)

constraints.name: BBN: additional relativistic dofs at T ~ MeV
constraints.description: Additional relativistic degrees of freedom during BBN modify expansion rate and element abundances.
constraints.observable: Delta N_eff from additional relativistic dofs during BBN
missing_observables: Evolution of dark sector temperature through PT and reheating
constraints.applicability: With xi=1, dark sector 4 dofs give Delta N_eff ~ 0.57, exceeding BBN bound. But after supercooled PT, residual abundance of massive particles may be negligible. Need to compute actual energy density distribution.
applicability_notes: The xi=1 assumption is likely violated after the supercooled PT
Need to compute post-PT dark sector temperature and density

source.title: Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters

constraints.name: NA62 dark photon visible decay A' -> mu+mu-
constraints.description: NA62 searched for A' -> mu+mu- in beam-dump mode with 400 GeV protons. Excludes 215-550 MeV mass range.
constraints.observable: epsilon vs m_A' from NA62 beam dump mu+mu- search

conventions.epsilon: 90% CL limits from 1.4e17 protons on dump (2021 data). Visible decay search.

constraints.applicability: Partially overlaps 1-sigma mA' range (0.31-0.55 GeV). Covers lower end. Upper end (0.55-0.67 GeV) not covered.
applicability_notes: NA62 results complementary to LHCb prompt search
Only visible mu+mu- channel probed

source.title: Search for dark photon decays to mu+mu- at NA62

constraints.name: KLOE-2 dark photon constraints
constraints.description: KLOE-2 search for dark photons in e+e- -> U gamma, U -> pi+pi- in the rho-omega mass region.
constraints.observable: epsilon from e+e- -> U gamma, U -> pi+pi-
constraints.applicability: Not directly applicable: KLOE-2 probes a narrow mass window that overlaps only a small part of our range. LHCb gives stronger constraints over the full range.
applicability_notes: LHCb provides stronger constraints across this entire mass range

source.title: KLOE-2 dark photon search in pi+pi- gamma final state

constraints.name: Supernova gamma-ray constraints on dark photon
constraints.description: Gamma rays from A' decays outside SN1987A constrain dark photon parameter space.
constraints.observable: Prompt and diffuse gamma rays from SN1987A from A' decays

conventions.epsilon: Gamma-ray constraints apply if A' is produced in SN core and decays outside emitting gamma rays.

constraints.applicability: Not applicable: m_A'=300-700 MeV production in SN core is Boltzmann-suppressed.
applicability_notes: Production suppressed by exp(-m_A'/T_core) with T_core ~ 30-60 MeV

source.title: High-energy neutrino signals from supernova explosions: a new window into dark photon parameter space

constraints.name: Dark rho production via Higgs mixing at LEP/LHC
constraints.description: Search for rho produced via Higgs mixing in rare meson decays or exotic Higgs decays.
constraints.observable: Exotic Higgs decays h -> rho+rho; rare meson decays (B, K)

conventions.sin^2(theta): Scalar-Higgs mixing. CMS probes h->SS for m_S > 0.4 GeV.

constraints.applicability: m_rho = 50-71 MeV is below CMS h->SS sensitivity threshold (0.4 GeV). Kaon decay experiments (NA62, E949) cover complementary range. Recast needed.
applicability_notes: CMS EXO-24-034 (2025): h->SS->mu+mu- h+h- for m_S=0.4-2 GeV -> m_rho too light
NA62, E949 constrain K->pi+invisible, covering m_rho < m_K - m_pi
LEP constraint: sin^2(theta) < 0.1 from Z lineshape

source.title: CMS search for light scalars via the Higgs portal (2025)

constraints.name: Structure formation constraints on warm dark matter
constraints.description: Lyman-alpha forest constraints on light thermal relic dark matter.
constraints.observable: Lyman-alpha forest power spectrum constraints
constraints.applicability: Not applicable: the model has no DM candidate. The rho and A' are unstable and decay via portal couplings.
applicability_notes: Model contains no fermionic DM candidate
Bosonic particles decay via portals

source.title: No DM candidate in model

constraints.name: Dark scalar freeze-in and relic overproduction
constraints.description: The dark scalar rho is produced via Higgs mixing portal. For large mixing, it thermalizes and freeze-out overproduces relic density. For small mixing, freeze-in yield must decay before BBN/CMB.
constraints.observable: Yield Y_S from freeze-in via Higgs mixing

conventions.sin^2(theta): For sin^2(theta) > 10^{-6}, rho thermalizes. Freeze-in yield Y_S ~ 3e11 * theta^2 for smaller mixing.

constraints.applicability: sin^2(theta) > 10^{-6} leads to thermal freeze-out and overproduction -> excluded. For 10^{-12} < sin^2(theta) < 10^{-7}, freeze-in produces subdominant population that must decay before BBN/CMB.
applicability_notes: Thermalization threshold: sin^2(theta) ~ 10^{-6} for scalar masses ~ 100 MeV
Need to compute freeze-in abundance to set CMB/BBN constraints precisely
Decoupling temperature of dark sector from SM determines xi evolution

source.title: Cosmological beam dump: constraints on dark scalars mixed with the Higgs boson

Proper treatment of hadronic decays via R-ratio

missing_calculations.observable: A' branching fractions (SM vs dark sector)

validation_checks: Total width includes all open SM channels
Exclusive hadronic decays via chiral perturbation theory

missing_calculations.observable: Dark photon decay width to SM (Gamma_SM)
validation_checks: Correct R-ratio interpolation for m_A'=300-700 MeV
Proper inclusion of rho, omega resonances

missing_calculations.observable: Dark sector temperature evolution and xi(T)
validation_checks: Boltzmann equations for energy density evolution
